Hollow Knight is genuinely good as a Metroidvania. The map design, progression, and exploration are strong, and if you mainly care about movement, upgrades, and that "keep pushing deeper" feeling, it delivers.  Some things of what people praise about it didn’t work for me. The music never really clicked. It’s not bad, but it never made areas feel memorable to me, most of the time it leans into the same nostalgic vibe with piano and strings. My biggest issue is with the story and lore. It often feels cryptic just for the sake of being cryptic, like it wants to be mysterious even when being clearer would make it more engaging.   The reason I didn’t rate it badly is simple. As a Metroidvania, the core gameplay is well made.

Lo que más amo de este juego es cómo logra combinar arte, música y jugabilidad de una forma que parece imposible. Cada zona de Hallownest tiene una identidad propia, desde sus paisajes hasta sus habitantes. Hay lugares que transmiten tranquilidad, otros misterio y otros una profunda melancolía. Simplemente caminar por el reino ya es una experiencia. La música es algo que nunca voy a olvidar. Cada canción parece contar una historia incluso sin palabras. Hay momentos en los que una melodía puede hacerte sentir paz, emoción o tristeza mientras exploras. La banda sonora no acompaña el juego: forma parte de él. Y luego está la jugabilidad. Los controles son precisos, los combates son emocionantes y cada jefe representa un reto que te obliga a mejorar. Cuando finalmente derrotas a un enemigo que te costó horas vencer, la satisfacción es enorme. Nunca sentí que el juego fuera injusto; siempre me hacía querer intentarlo una vez más. Hollow Knight me hizo perderme en su mundo, descubrir secretos, emocionarme con sus personajes y admirar el cariño que sus creadores pusieron en cada detalle. Es uno de esos juegos que te hacen recordar por qué te gustan los videojuegos. Si nunca lo has jugado, te estás perdiendo una de las experiencias más hermosas que existen en este medio. Y si le das una oportunidad, quizá entiendas por qué tantas personas consideran a Hollow Knight una obra maestra.

Es una experiencia, puede sonar cliché, pero lo es. Perderse en el mundo de Hollow Knight, que a pesar de ser subterraneo sabe ofrecer entornos muy variados, con un apartado sonoro muy pulido y una historia que puedes seguir explorando y consiguiendole nuevos giros durante un buen tiempo es realmente un gusto.  La jugabilidad a veces es super relajante y otras super tensa. Pero en esos momentos en que estás en estado de flow y te sientes uno con el juego, olvidandote un poco de lo demás, se te vuelan las horas. Yo lo jugué en mis vacaciones, y fue lo correcto, porque en mi dia a dia normal no suelo tener más de 30minutos o 1 hora para jugar, dia de por medio, y este juego lo disfrute más con sesiones largas.  Es una experiencia recomendada para todo aquel que guste de un mundo vivo, una buena historia, una jugabilidad retadora y un juego con capacidad de estresarte y relajarte al mismo tiempo.  Lo super recomiendo.

Positives:  - Map design  - Music  - Graphics  - Story  - Charms being mostly balanced and have build variety (heal vs spell vs basic attack)  - Has dream gate that avoids long runbacks  - Normal enemies and gauntlets are easy so I didn't hit a wall during exploration. Negatives:  - Most boss fights are boring. They require me to do the same maneuver repeatedly. Some endgame bosses are good.  - White Palace is a huge difficulty spike at platforming. I expected some mild challenges like Silksong's Cogwork Core.  - An early game area's map is locked behind a late game ability. Having to navigate without a map sucks.

[h1]Hollow Knight: uma aventura que vale cada minuto[/h1]  [b]Hollow Knight é aquele jogo que faz você entrar em uma caverna por curiosidade e sair horas depois sem nem lembrar qual era seu objetivo inicial.[/b]  O mundo é enorme, cheio de caminhos escondidos, áreas secretas e detalhes que fazem a exploração ser tão recompensadora quanto o combate. A cada nova habilidade, você percebe que existem dezenas de lugares que ainda não conseguiu alcançar.  [h2]Explorar é tão importante quanto lutar[/h2]  Grande parte da experiência está em descobrir a história do mundo por conta própria e encontrar personagens estranhos que parecem ter saído de um sonho. O jogo raramente pega sua mão, e isso faz cada descoberta parecer realmente sua.  [h2]Chefes que ficam na memória[/h2]  O grande diferencial de Hollow Knight é como ele transforma desafio em satisfação. Os chefes são marcantes, exigem que você aprenda seus movimentos e melhore como jogador, não apenas que consiga equipamentos melhores.  Quando você finalmente derrota um chefe que parecia impossível, a sensação é de conquista de verdade.  [h2]Por que tanta gente ama esse jogo?[/h2]  Porque ele consegue fazer você se perder, sofrer, explorar e se impressionar ao mesmo tempo. Poucos jogos deixam uma lembrança tão forte depois que terminam.

Just finished my first playthrough of this game and I figured I HAVE to leave a review considering how much I have enjoyed this adventure through the might Hallownest.  This review will be completely SPOILER-FREE so I won't ruin anyone's first exprience with this.  First of all, this is NOT an easy game, I found myself rather stressed at times where I couldn't traverse certain areas because of the insane amount of enemies there or when fighting different tough bosses. It is a game strongly focused on trial and error so if you don't like dying too much in video games, well you'll have quite a time here ;)  Visually, the game is absolutely stunning, the artstyle is rather cute and friendly at times, but it can get gross and kinda disturbing at times too. The environment and the backgrounds of every place inside this game is absolutely breath-taking, not even starting to talk about the INSANE size of the map  I figured lots of people tend to drop this game because it is not one of those games where you just have a pre-marked location with a waypoint and you just have to go there, the entire game is strictly based on exploration and it counts on YOU to unravel it's secrets. If you're a person that loves exploring, this is a great game for you. You will, however, meet lots of places that you can't pass through until you get a special ability to do so, there are a bunch of them and each one of them does something unique. So yeah, there IS a lot of backtracking and remembering stuff you discover. But hey, that is the metroidvania genre! ;)  I had such a blast with this game and I look forward to buying the sequel as well. It can get really frustrating at times, but I 100% recommend pushing through it and experiencing this to it's very end.

Si hay un juego que demuestra que no hace falta un presupuesto gigantesco para crear una obra maestra, ese es Hollow Knight.  Desde los primeros minutos, el juego te atrapa con su atmósfera melancólica, su increíble dirección artística dibujada a mano y una banda sonora que convierte cada zona en una experiencia memorable. El reino de Hallownest se siente vivo, misterioso y lleno de secretos; explorar cada rincón da una sensación de aventura que pocos juegos logran transmitir.  El combate es simple de entender pero difícil de dominar. Cada jefe representa un desafío justo, obligándote a mejorar tus reflejos y aprender patrones en lugar de depender de mejoras exageradas. La satisfacción de derrotar a un enemigo que te costó varios intentos es enorme.  Uno de los aspectos más impresionantes es la cantidad de contenido. Por un precio relativamente bajo, ofrece decenas de horas de exploración, combates, misiones secundarias y secretos. Muchos juegos modernos con presupuestos mucho mayores ofrecen menos contenido.  Sin embargo, no es un juego perfecto. Su principal crítica es que puede resultar confuso para algunos jugadores. En ciertos momentos no queda claro hacia dónde avanzar, y perderse es parte de la experiencia. Además, algunas caminatas para volver a enfrentar jefes pueden sentirse largas y frustrantes.  Aun así, estas críticas son pequeñas frente a todo lo que hace bien. Hollow Knight recompensa la curiosidad, la perseverancia y la exploración de una forma que pocos títulos consiguen. Es un juego que respeta la inteligencia del jugador y que deja recuerdos incluso mucho tiempo después de terminarlo.  Calificación: 9.5/10  Si te gustan los desafíos, la exploración y los mundos llenos de secretos, Hollow Knight es una compra casi obligatoria. Es de esos juegos que hacen que cada peso gastado valga la pena y que fácilmente pueden convertirse en uno de tus favoritos de todos los tiempos.

Hollow Knight is a side-scrolling adventure game built around exploration and movement through connected areas. The visual style is hand-drawn and detailed, with a dark tone that gives each region a distinct identity.   There is very little direct instruction on where to go, so progress mainly comes from paying attention to surroundings and slowly learning how different paths connect. At first it can feel unclear, but over time the layout starts to make more sense as new routes open after gaining movement abilities.  Movement between areas often requires returning to earlier places once new skills are obtained, which changes how older sections are viewed. Each region has its own map structure that has to be uncovered step by step, and locating mapping points becomes important for keeping track of routes.   The combat is based on timing and positioning rather than simple attacks, with enemies following different patterns that require adjustment in approach. It is a highly engaging experience that rewards patience and exploration, and is a strong choice for those who like interconnected world design and skill-based progression.  I played the game using keyboard and mouse despite having an Xbox controller, because I personally prefer keyboard and mouse controls, even though I assume most players would choose a controller. To check and set up the keyboard and mouse binds, a screenshot of the layout is attached below as a reference for keyboard and mouse players.  Overall, the game offers a large world with many hidden routes, optional paths, and multiple possible outcomes based on how much is discovered. The control system works well and allows precise movement during both platform sections and battles. It stands out as a well-crafted experience with attention to detail and steady exploration throughout the entire journey from start to finish.  https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3733128589

🗡️ Hollow Knight – Recensione Completa (8/10) 🐞  Hollow Knight è uno dei metroidvania più apprezzati degli ultimi anni. Con il suo mondo affascinante, l'atmosfera malinconica e un gameplay solido, riesce a offrire un'avventura memorabile che conquista fin dalle prime ore. ✨  🌍 Un mondo affascinante da esplorare Il regno di Hallownest è enorme, misterioso e pieno di segreti.  ✔ Ambientazioni varie e curate ✔ Tante aree nascoste da scoprire ✔ Atmosfera unica e suggestiva  L'esplorazione è senza dubbio uno degli aspetti migliori del gioco. Ogni nuova zona riesce a incuriosire e spinge a continuare l'avventura. 🏰  ⚔️ Gameplay semplice ma efficace I controlli sono precisi e il sistema di combattimento funziona molto bene.  🔥 Schivate rapide 🔥 Combattimenti fluidi 🔥 Abilità che si sbloccano durante l'avventura  Con il passare delle ore il personaggio diventa sempre più forte e versatile, rendendo l'esplorazione ancora più soddisfacente.  👑 Boss Fight I boss rappresentano alcune delle sfide migliori del gioco.  ✔ Design memorabili ✔ Combattimenti impegnativi ✔ Grande varietà di approcci  Alcuni scontri possono risultare difficili, ma regalano una bella soddisfazione quando vengono superati. 💪  🎵 Colonna Sonora e Atmosfera La musica accompagna perfettamente l'avventura. 🎶  Le tracce sono delicate, malinconiche e riescono a rendere Hallownest ancora più affascinante.  😬 I Difetti Nonostante le sue qualità, qualche limite c'è:  ❌ Alcune sezioni possono risultare dispersive ❌ Backtracking a volte eccessivo ❌ Ritmo non sempre costante  In certi momenti si può passare molto tempo a cercare la strada giusta, rischiando di rallentare l'esperienza.  ❤️ Cosa funziona meglio ✔ Mondo di gioco magnifico ✔ Atmosfera unica ✔ Esplorazione coinvolgente ✔ Boss fight ben realizzate  🏁 Conclusione Hollow Knight è un metroidvania di grande qualità, capace di offrire decine di ore di esplorazione e sfide in un mondo affascinante e ricco di misteri.  🎯 Voto finale: 8/10  Molto bello, coinvolgente e ricco di personalità. Non perfetto in ogni aspetto, ma resta un'avventura che vale assolutamente la pena vivere. 🐞🗡️✨

I've played the first 4 Metroid 2D titles before, and I find that Hollow Knight is more difficult than them in some of it ways. I really enjoyed the atmosphere that game built. Although most of the bosses (even mini-bosses) are difficult, I kind of enjoy fighting them, except for a few. And for the maps and obstacles, it require intermediate to expert "Metroidvania" proficiency. I really enjoyed the areas and the "platforming" aspect of this game.  My main problem would be the unclear linear-progression backbone of this game. While the environment shows one thing or two about you should explore it later (backtrack), sometimes I kind of lost and overwhelmed of what should I do next.  Compared to Metroid games that have comprehensive environment for backtracking and all in my opinion.  Basically, there's too much content being put on non-linearly. I guess that is why most of casual players quit the game. But overall, it's an impressive game and I really like it.

Ahem...   When I first started playing Hollow Knight, I was very bad at it.  Very. Bad.  However, this did not deter me, and little by little I continued onward throughout the game, slowly getting better at the platforming, at the combat, the fact that it was  metrovania, a game genre I didn't know existed, I had just heard good things about it and wanted to try something I'd never tried before.  While traveling through the world, I discovered a bug being eaten by an enormous, flying creature. I knew I had to save him, so after far too many tries, I saved him... I saved Zote. I expected gratitude, a thank you, or perhaps some cocky wanna be hero telling me he had it under control... I got the last one, but it was... ruder than I ever could have imagined... insulting, degrading, just a horrible, horrible bug... yet, I was fond of this horrible little creature. "Perhaps I'll encounter him again, and I'll be able to help him change into a better bug on my journey!" I thought to myself, and happily continued on my way, happy to have saved a life.  Some time later, and after much more improvement, I found a hidden passage way in the Fungal wastes, and within it, at the top of what at the time was a very difficult platforming challenge for me, I saw a lost, scared, bug, clearly on the verge of being taken by the infection. I kept trying, and trying, and trying to traverse this not even difficult challenge, determined to save this bug, whoever they were, and return them to safety, and eventually, I did... I saved Bretta.  Instantly I fell in love with Bretta (not romantically of course), she was so sweet, so shy, so lost, feeling completely and utterly forgotten, yet here I was, and I was now her only friend.  Continuing on with my travels, I would frequently stop into Dirtmouth, and would always check on Bretta, for I didn't want her to feel alone, nor forgotten. Her infatuation with Ghost was honestly endearing to me, for she was never weird about it, she was never obsessive, nor odd, she was just shy, sweet, and quiet, even when her diary entries offered a completely different side of her. Even if her stories were exaggerated, even if they weren't real, I always looked forward to reading the next entry, for it was nice seeing someone so real within that small town. Dare I think it, I wanted to be the white knight she saw me as. Visiting Bretta became a wonderful tradition for me, and sitting beside her on the bench gave me a huge sense of connection to the world, a reason to always come back to Dirtmouth.  Then I went into Deepnest.  There, I once again discovered Zote in peril, and once again I rushed in to save him, hoping that perhaps this time he'd show a bit of character growth, or even a half baked attempt at a thank you. I did not receive this, I instead received insults, degradation, and rudeness. Still though, I just rolled my eyes, and thought to myself, "Oh Zote, when will you learn?" Thus, I continued on, happy to have saved a life again.  Much later, once I was in the Colosseum of Fools, I once again found Zote, though this time locked in a cage, with once again nothing but insults to offer me. I laughed to myself, wished him luck, and entered the Challenge of the Warrior. I was mortified when at the end a huge, shaking cage dropped from above, only to burst into laughter when Zote appeared, waving his wooden sword pathetically and tripping on his own feet. Reluctantly, for I was worried I'd kill him, I did fight him, and sent him flying, sticking him in the ground by his own horns.  "Oh Zote," I thought to myself, "when will you learn?" Thus, I continued on with my journeys, wondering when Zote would change...  Then I got back to Dirtmouth.  I expected to see Bretta sitting on the bench, or perhaps sleeping in her room, a new diary entry ready to be read, a new story about her white knight just waiting to be seen. I was so looking forward to seeing her again, it truly had been a long time, and I looked forward to our silent time together...  What did I find?  ZOTE.  I found ZOTE, rambling on so loudly that the whole town could hear it, with Bretta... My dear, sweet, shy Bretta, blushing, listening to his monstrous, horrible, inflated lies, completely and utterly enamored.  My world went black.  Instantly I rushed into her room, only to find it had been overtaken by ZOTE. ZOTE not filled her journal, while any mentions of me were dashed to pieces by this horrible creatures lies, framing me as a cowardly villain within his own story. Images of ZOTE now filled her walls, the plushy of Ghost now replaced with something far more grotesque, more annoying, more... ZOTE.  Just when all hope seemed lost, I then found it... a basement within Bretta's home... which held a great, paper mache stature of ZOTE with dream particles coming off of it.  This was it, if I could beat this, I could rid myself of ZOTE.  I quickly entered the battle, needle drawn and ready, prepared to face on whatever horrors awaited me...  Or so I thought...  I wasn't expecting to see Bretta atop a bed, being fanned and served by ZOTE'S... nor did I expect the GREY PRINCE ZOTE.  I'll not go into detail, I simply cannot describe it, truly, it was... grotesque.  However, in the end I vanquished the enemy, and upon emerging victorious, I quickly ran back to find Bretta, ready to hear her tell off Zote, to kick him out of the town, to find herself and her confidence!  Instead, she now looked rather uninfatuated, bored even, with her once Grey Prince. Liking what I saw, but confused all the same, I returned to the basement, only to find that the dream particles were still there. Knowing what I had to do, I returned into that wretched abyss, ready to fight him however many times I needed to to win her back. I battled against ZOTE four times, each battle becoming more and more unfathomable, truly, grotesque in every way imaginable.  When I earned my fourth victory, I emerged, hoping to find my Bretta, hoping to discover ZOTE gone, or being told off, something, anything!  What I found instead shattered me to my very core.  I found Bretta gone, and Zote still rambling on loudly to no one at all.  Panicked, I searched her diary for answers, and to my horror discovered that she had indeed awakened from ZOTE'S spell... and left Hollownest entirely because of it in order to truly find herself.  Gone.  My dear, sweet, shy Bretta was gone...  NOW REPLACED WITH ZOTE!!!!!!!!  I did everything I could to make him leave, I listened to all his precepts, I fought his dream form until I simply couldn't do it anymore, I did EVERYTHING!!!  But he just wouldn't leave.  I was stuck with him, rambling for the whole town to hear, for the rest of my playthrough...  While Bretta was gone forever.  I was heartbroken.  ...  I've since started my second playthrough, and once again found myself staring up at ZOTE, being chewed on by that great, wonderful vengefly. I could have saved him, I'm better now, it wouldn't have been a problem, I'm a great player.   Why on earth would I do that though, when I can keep Bretta safe in Dirtmouth, and perhaps more importantly, safe from ZOTE? When I can keep Dirtmouth safe from ZOTE?  Ladies and gentlemen, non-binary friends, do you know why you should play Hollow Knight? The real reason you should play Hollow Knight?  It's so that you can get the NEGLECT achievement, and truly, truly, keep Dirtmouth, and Hollownest for that matter as well as all it's inhabitants safe from that horrible, rude, insulting, lying, terrible, monstrous, ugly, belittling, manipulating, stupid, rambling, annoying, insidious bug known as ZOTE.  Thank you for your time.

melhor jogu qe eu já joguei na minha vidah!!!!!!! abandonei us estudoss, terminei o namoru, zerei as notas da escola, parei de durmir, deichei de sair de casa, isolêi minhas relações sóciais para poder jolgar eze jogo! Todo dia! Toda hora! Compri ese jogo duás veses!!!!!!!!!!!!!! È INCRÌVELLLL!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!    Falando sério agora:   Sobre os gráficos: absolutamente majestosamente, incrivelmente, brilhantemente, cinematograficamente, criticamente, honestamente, geniosamente INCRÍVEIS! É TUDO LINDO, PERFEITO, CADA TRAÇO, SOMBRA, BRILHO, CURVA, PONTA, CHÃO, PAREDE, CRIATURA, DETALHE, C A D A  COISA EM SEU DEVIDO LUGAR DE UM JEITO SURPREENDENTEMENTE NATURAL!   História/Lore: Sim. Muito boa. O jogo conta a lore de um jeito sutil, leve e misterioso de uma forma perfeita, como se aquele universo tivesse se criado sozinho, espalhando segredos e detalhes em lugares escondidos e ocultos aguardando serem encontrados, quase como um documento, uma memória, uma recordação do passado de um reino em lástimas, que em seu tempo de glória, deixou um legado fascinante e de certa forma cruel e impiedoso com aqueles que ousassem desenterrá-lo. Corpos, memórias, almas, escrituras, pinturas, ruínas. Cada pequeno detalhe escondendo uma peça de um grande quebra-cabeça esperando ser completado para, só então, decifrar a grande imagem que representa os grandes e também muito pequeninos segredos de Hallownest. Mas ter lore misteriosa não adiantaria se fosse uma lore RUIM! Que não é o caso, por que toda a história desta pérola é incrível! Não vou entrar em detalhes por questão de spoilers  Música: Sem palavras. Sério, não sei o que falar, eu REALMENTE não sei o que falar, não é por que eu achei incrível, é por que eu não sei o que falar. MAS... A TRILHA SONORA É INCRÍVEL!!  Jogabilidade: Amei! Difícil do jeitinho que eu gosto, feito para desafiar a coordenação motora do jogador! Claro que você pode mudar as keybinds, mas eu amei os controles padrão, do jeito que tá mesmo. Amei a interface do inventário, mapa, etc. É legal porquê dá a sensação de "Handmande", como se o cavaleiro tivesse usado suas pequenas mãozinhas de cotoco para desenhar a interface! O jeito que você completa o Diário do Caçador é fascinante (sem detalhes, também por causa de spoilers)!    Bem, acho que isso é tudo, espero que tenha gostado, quem quer que tenha lido isso. Hollow Knight ist für mich eines der besten Indie-Spiele überhaupt. Es ist wirklich beeindruckend, was ein so kleines Entwicklerteam hier auf die Beine gestellt hat. Das Spiel kann problemlos mit vielen großen Titeln und bekannten Spielestudios mithalten. Die Welt ist wunderschön gestaltet, voller Details und hat eine ganz besondere Atmosphäre. Jeder Bereich fühlt sich einzigartig an und lädt dazu ein, immer weiter zu erkunden. Dazu kommen ein großartiger Soundtrack und ein Artstyle, der einfach zeitlos wirkt. Man sollte aber wissen, dass Hollow Knight kein leichtes Spiel ist. Einige Kämpfe und Bossgegner können ziemlich fordernd sein und man braucht oft mehrere Anläufe, bis man weiterkommt. Wer schnell frustriert aufgibt, wird hier wahrscheinlich nicht glücklich. Wer jedoch Spiele wie Dark Souls oder generell anspruchsvolle Spiele mag, wird Hollow Knight lieben. Für den Preis bekommt man unglaublich viel Inhalt, eine fantastische Spielwelt und viele Stunden Spielspaß. Klare Empfehlung von mir. Ein echtes Meisterwerk und der beste Beweis dafür, dass man kein riesiges Budget braucht, um ein außergewöhnliches Spiel zu erschaffen.

[h1]总分：9.5/10[/h1] 游戏性：简洁的战斗系统，不需要过多的学习成本，黑冲，黑砸，黑吼加上多种护符的选择和搭配可以导向不同的流派，可以给10/10 剧情：碎片化叙事，因人而异，可以给9/10（ [spoiler]快出官方动画，出了就改成10/10 [/spoiler]） 美术风格：画风独特，美术和音乐都很符合我的口味，无可挑剔，10/10 引导：引导优秀，地图设计水平相当高，可以称为类恶魔银河城的标杆，9/10 总体内容：内容量相当充足，加上dlc，体量不小，可以给10/10

Hollow Knight is one of those rare games that completely pulled me in from start to finish. At first, I thought it was just another indie platformer, but the deeper I explored Hallownest, the more I realized how much care and passion went into every part of the game. The world feels alive despite being a fallen kingdom, with beautiful environments, memorable characters, and an atmosphere that constantly makes you want to see what's around the next corner.  The exploration is incredibly rewarding because the game doesn't hold your hand. Every new area feels like a genuine discovery, and finding hidden paths or secrets always gave me a sense of accomplishment. The combat is simple but satisfying, and the boss fights are some of the best I've experienced in a Metroidvania game. Many of them took me multiple attempts, but defeating them never felt unfair—it felt earned.  The soundtrack deserves special praise as well. It perfectly matches the mood of each area and made many moments even more memorable. While the game can be challenging and sometimes leaves you feeling lost, that's also part of what makes the adventure so special.  If you enjoy exploration, challenging combat, and games that trust players to figure things out on their own, Hollow Knight is an easy recommendation. It's easily one of the best indie games I've ever played and absolutely worth every hour I spent in it.

I played Silksong before I played this game, so I will try to not compare the two, as both are extraordinarily unique and offer different experiences for many players, including myself. After beating Silksong, I moved on to this game. Looking back, I wish I had beaten this game before playing Silksong, since I didn't get much of the references in [spoiler]the Red Memory[/spoiler] but I did have some cool moments where I saw pieces of the story lining up. Other than that, this game is extremely well made. For a metroidvania, I kind of like the more linear gameplay (in my opinion...) and raw story that this game offers. Hollow Knight has well balanced and expertly themed areas, enemies, and songs that provide an epic, serene quality and ambiance that this game deserves. Every single one of the bosses are amazing (besides Grey Defenders), but I'm not sweaty enough to do the pantheons. I think the only thing slightly odd is that [spoiler]Dung Defender[/spoiler] has a different art style than everything else. It just feels the tiny bit out of place, but at the same time, it makes the game even more classic. There are some parts that feel a little repetitive and dull, and platforms that feel more like a maze than parkour, but that shouldn't stop you at all from enjoying yourself. This game is phenomenal. I recommend.

«Мир жуков, где каждый имеет своё место и важен по отдельности».  Данная игра однозначно один из лучших (а может быть, и лучший) платформер, который существует на данный момент.  Сам геймплей просто шикарен: игроку никогда не становится скучно, потому что контента в игре ровно столько, сколько нужно, чтобы не заскучать. Помимо прочего, в этой замечательной игре есть ещё и сюжет. Пусть его порой и тяжело понять без внешних источников, но сам факт его наличия (а лор там далеко не маленький) уже заставляет радоваться.  Я считаю, что это чудо-творение от Team Cherry стоит пройти хоть раз на любую из концовок.  10 Атамасчио из 10 🪲🌱

А почему такой хайп вокруг этой игры?) Визуальный стиль - уныние какое-то в котором не хочется находиться. Музыка - максимально дженерик, не встретил ни одного трека, который бы хоть чем-то был примечателен. Механика платформинга, инерция прыжков - какая-то контринтуитивная хрень. Лор раскрывается по грамулечке в час, ждешь и ждешь, когда хоть что-то станет понятно. Отсутствие выбора уровня сложности - это конечно пик современного гейм дизайна. В общем, не понял за что вообще можно любить эту игру. Причем жанр метроидвании я люблю и прошел несколько других игр с удовольствием, но ХК это что-то на любителя.

Полый Рыцарь это та игра в которую ты хочешь играть снова и снова чтобы почувствовать ещё больше страданий почти сравнимых со всеми частями тёмных душ. Если вы думали что Хидэтака Миядзаки самый больной человек на земле любящий наблюдать за страданиями людей так вот к нему ещё присоединяются разработчики Team Cherry, эти гении создали отдельный режим стальной души, что значит пройти игру без смертей и добавили целых два достижения связанные с эти режимом и если ты любитель платин то готовься надорвать свою жопу в попытках не умереть на паркур стадии или от кого то мелкого жука который даже не собирался тебя трогать. В этой прекрасной игре нет места казуалам поскольку если ты захочешь пройти 5 пантеон тебе потребуется победить 42 босса подряд без смертей а если сдохнешь то будешь начинать всё сначала). Это детище выжмет из тебя все соки заставив перепроходить на другие концовки, собирать всё что не собрал при первом прохождении и покажет на сколько насекомые уёбищные создания по жизни. Если вы никогда не играли в соулс лайки или другие проекты связанные с прокачкой своего персонажа и последующей долбёжкой боссов то советую начать с Полого Рыцаря поскольку являясь наглядным примером хорошей метроидвании он заставит вас запатеть и окунаться в прекрасный мир страданий, боли и не понимания почему жуки такие сильные суки. Why did it take me so long to play this game? I don't know. Do I regret not playing it sooner? Absolutely.   I you like metroidvanias then you will like this one. It quite literally is a flagship game for the genre alongside metriod.  Pros: Movement is fantastic. The music and atmosphere is on point. The art needs to be in a museum. The build options that can be done with the charms is incredible. The in game maps are done very well and the map markers in this edition are well done as well. The atmospheric storytelling that is done is also fantastic. I'm just waiting for a good enough sale to buy Silksong now!  Cons: Essentially zero direction for what to do and where you need to go. I spent several hours on google and Reddit trying to understand what I needed to do to get the 118% completion. Also, some of the later game bosses were much more difficult than the final fight (If not doing true ending) And some of the pantheon fights and radiance fights are incredibly unfair and almost impossible to beat. (Granted they are 100% optional and supposed to be a challenge but for some of them, not getting hit is essentially impossible unlike path of pain which is just really difficult).

Тот самая игра, в которой в свое время аутировал папич. Известная во многих кругах, классная по атмосфере, обязательная к покупке как основа жанра.   Геймплей не напряжный, локации саунд и тд. Из плюсов можно отметить не душную боевку и классные локации. А из минусов, душную систему возврата в локации - где ты не мог раньше попасть, мне как деду с деменцией это сложно. И второе - паркур части в локациях.  В остальном же у игры больше плюсов чем минусов, просто игра как данность, в которую каждый должен поиграть. На DualSence ощущается отлично.

Este jogo é um tutorial de como fazer um jogo com poucas pessoa, como jogar: -Entre no jogo -ande com o hollow -cai no espinho -tenta de novo -cai de novo -vc consegue -morre para um bichinho -vc spawna novamente -vc mata seu fantasma -depois de 15 horas vc mata o hollow knight só para descobrir a radiança -vc morre (mais de 200x) -vc mata a radiança -descobre que existe finais alternativos -descobre um torneio de bosses -vc da ragekit e desinstala o game 10/10 recomendo para todos

Oyun inanılmaz iyi, hikaye, oynanış, zorluk bakımından mükemmel. Bazen bir boss'a 10 kez veya daha fazla kaybetmeme rağmen yüzümde bir gülümseme var ve tekrar savaşmak için sabırsızlanıyorum. Müzikleri harika, savaşlara renk katıyor. Primal Aspid bile sinirimi bozmuyor bu oyunda. Oyunu %112 bitirmiş biri olarak tek söyleyebileceğim şey "Godhome"da çalan müzikler. Eski savaştığım bosslarda onların tema şarkıları çalsın istiyorum. Bence Pantheon'daki müzik güzel değil, her farklı boss çıktığında onun tema müziği çalmalı. Sevgili Team Cherry, lütfen Silksong'a Pantheon getirdiğiniz zaman yeni garip müzikler türetmeyin, oyunun içerisindeki müzikler zaten mükemmel, onları kullanırsanız oyuncu bir dejavu yaşar, daha keyifli oynar.

Впечатления от игры очень неоднозначные. Сама игра неплохая, красивые локации, прикольные элементы платформинга, интересная задумка, отдельно мне понравились боссы, в особенности Чистый Сосуд и Лучезарность, сражение с ними доставили мне настоящее удовольствие, но на этом наверное плюсы заканчиваются.  Боёвка работает по принципу "ударил - отбежал", что само по себе не плохо, но к концу игры начинает изрядно надоедать. Сюжет в таких играх не играет ключевую роль, но было бы неплохо чтобы после кучи загадочных посланий и намёков нам в конце раскрыли побольше лора, концовки так вообще эту задачу совершенно не выполняют и на самом деле хз для чего существуют, вероятно чтобы вкинуть намёк на сиквел, что вышел через 9 лет.  Вот что в игре действительно плохо, так это существование Пантеонов, в особенности пятого. В чем прикол заставлять игрока сражаться со всеми боссами подряд, чуть ли не досконально их заучивая, тратя на это примерно 1/3 времени которое потребовалось на полное прохождение игры? При том что лорно важность пантеонов никак не объяснена, и в целом не имеет для него никакой важности.  Хотелось бы ещё пару слов добавить о ачивках типо прохождения игры на 100% на стальной душе, и прохождении игры на 100% за 20 часов, которые требуют ещё часов 20 на их получение.  Короче главная претензия в том, что прохождение самой игры занимает условных часов 70, а закрытие перечисленных выше 3-х ачивок занимает ещё примерно часов 30-40, полностью перебивая какие-либо приятные ощущения от игры. Воистину одна из самых лучших игр за всё время. В ней идеально всё , шикарный баланс (рукожоп вроде меня прошёл в лёгкую) , отличный и удобный геймплей , движения и увороты чувствуются идеально,без всяких задержек. Мир - это отдельная тема , его интересно исследовать , аж настолько, что под конец я открыл почти всё ( открыто 84%) и на оставшееся плюнул, т.к. вкачан был по самые яйца. Боссы в принципе все лёгкие , быстро привыкаешь к ним (я максимум потратил около 10 траев на одного из боссов, и то потому что рановато пришёл) . Отдельно радует,что боссы не душнят и находятся всегда недалеко (на крайняк есть тп в любую точку карты) . В ДС например битвы с боссами - это то ещё удовольствие, то удар не прошёл, то модельку каким-то боком задело , то камера улетела хрен пойми куда . Тут такого нет , на 2-3-4 попытке ты уже точно изучишь все атаки босса и привыкнешь к ним. Отыграл очень много часов и мне всё равно мало , показалось короткой , хотя по таймингу так не скажешь.  Много времени потерял из-за карты , один нюанс долго не понимал, и это единственный мало-мизерный минус . В остальном - это шедевр , в который должен сыграть каждый , и обязательно купить для поддержки разрабов .

我认为空洞骑士是类银河战士恶魔城史上最伟大的作品，但这款游戏并不适合所有人入坑，如果你觉得这个游戏的所有缺点你都能接受，那剩下的就全部是优点了。假设我以一个从没玩过类银甚至平台横板卷轴类游戏的普通玩家的视角来看，以下为游戏的部分缺点： 1.前期0引导。与其说这是空洞骑士的缺点，不如说这是大部分的类银都有的问题，0引导会导致你在游戏的前期根本不知道去哪探索，很容易逛着逛着就对游戏失去兴趣 2.剧情极致碎片。碎片到如果你是一个普通玩家，到打完结局，你可能都不知道游戏到底讲了什么。 3.游戏难度偏高。如果你此前从未接触过任何大众眼中的高难度游戏，那么对你来说，空洞骑士可能并不是适应2D高难度游戏的合适选择，你应当选择一些难度曲线更加平滑，更适合新手玩家的游戏。 4.地图很容易迷路。在不查找攻略的情况下，只靠一张地图其实是很容易迷路的，这会让你对游戏失去兴趣。  可能我的观点比较片面，但对于普通玩家来说，以上我所提出的缺点应该是客观存在的。但综合来说，我的评价还是：推荐

It's one of those games where the atmosphere makes you feel like you are in the world itself. The game is exceptionally difficult but in the way that makes your heart crave for another try at that platforming section or boss. The lore and story can be hard to decipher though so be sure to read all the dialogue and text, and let it sit on your mind. 10/10
